papers.title: Appearance Decomposition Gaussian Splatting for Multi-Traversal Reconstruction
papers.abstract_snippet: Multi-traversal scene reconstruction is important for high-fidelity autonomous driving simulation and digital twin construction. This task involves integrating multiple sequences captured from the same geographical area at different times. In this context, a primary challenge is the significant appearance inconsistency across traversals caused by varying illumination and environmental conditions, despite the shared underlying geometry. papers.reason: 专注于自动驾驶仿真和数字孪生的场景重建，属于纯视觉/图形学领域，无机器人操作或 VLA 控制策略。

papers.title: ICR-Drive: Instruction Counterfactual Robustness for End-to-End Language-Driven Autonomous Driving
papers.abstract_snippet: Recent progress in vision-language-action (VLA) models has enabled language-conditioned driving agents to execute natural-language navigation commands in closed-loop simulation, yet standard evaluations largely assume instructions are precise and well-formed. In deployment, instructions vary in phrasing and specificity, may omit critical qualifiers, and can occasionally include misleading, authority-framed text, leaving instruction-level robustness under-measured. papers.reason: 针对语言驱动驾驶代理的指令鲁棒性研究，虽涉及 VLA 概念但局限于自动驾驶仿真，缺乏通用机器人操作验证。

papers.title: Belief Dynamics for Detecting Behavioral Shifts in Safe Collaborative Manipulation
papers.authors: Devashri Naik, Divake Kumar, Nastaran Darabi, Amit Ranjan Trivedi
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2604.04967v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Robots operating in shared workspaces must maintain safe coordination with other agents whose behavior may change during task execution. papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2604.04974v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Video is a scalable observation of physical dynamics: it captures how objects move, how contact unfolds, and how scenes evolve under interaction -- all without requiring robot action labels.
